description: This diagram illustrates the geometric setup of a satellite-borne radar remote sensing system observing the Earth. A satellite, depicted as a blue rectangle with dimensions L and D, travels along an "Orbit" with a defined "Satellite velocity". From the satellite, microwave energy is emitted along a "Line Of Sight (range)" towards the Earth's surface. The vertical distance from the satellite to the Earth is labeled "H". The "Off-nadir angle" (θ) is shown as the angle between the vertical (nadir) direction and the Line Of Sight. The radar beam has an angular spread denoted by "α", which creates an illuminated area on the Earth's surface referred to as the "Swath". The illuminated area has a "ground range" dimension perpendicular to the satellite's path and extends in the "azimuth" direction, which is parallel to the satellite's velocity vector. The "Antenna footprint in azimuth direction" is specified as (βa x R), where βa is the azimuth beamwidth and R is the range. The diagram shows the sensor's perspective of capturing data over a wide swath on the Earth's surface.
